nodes.notes: This toolCalculator node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Create New Time Entry

parameters.toolDescription: Call this workflow whenever you need to work with Clockify for create a new time entry.

values.name: logDescription
values.description: Description of the time log entry

values.name: endTime
values.description: Represents an end date of the time log in yyyy-MM-ddThh:mm:ssZ format.

values.name: projectId
values.description: Represents project unique identifier across the system.

values.name: startTime
values.description: Represents a start date of the time log in yyyy-MM-ddThh:mm:ssZ format.

nodes.notes: This toolHttpRequest node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: GetClientsTool

parameters.toolDescription: Call this tool whenever you need to get or filter clients on Clockify, especially if you need to find client id by name.

values.name: name
values.description: Filters client results that matches with the string provided in their client name.

nodes.notes: This toolHttpRequest node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Get All Time Entries

parameters.toolDescription:
Call this tool whenever you need to get time entries on Clockify for a given user identified by its user Id (not name).
By default fetch entries from the last month if no dates are provided.

values.name: startTime
values.description: Represents start date in yyyy-MM-ddThh:mm:ssZ format. Optional

values.name: endTime
values.description: Represents end date in yyyy-MM-ddThh:mm:ssZ format. Optional

values.name: userId
values.description: Id of the user that is logged in, for which we are retrieving time logs

nodes.notes: This toolHttpRequest node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Current loggedin user

parameters.toolDescription: Gel the current logged in user that you are operating from. Use it to get user profikle information, especially its id, email, name etc.

nodes.notes: This toolHttpRequest node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: GetProjectsTool

parameters.toolDescription:
Call this tool whenever you need to get projects on Clockify. 
This is a tool to use if you want to find all projects and project ids for the logged in user.

Optionally you can filter projects by clients or by project names.

values.name: projectName
values.description: Project name; not project ID; not client ID; can be empty

values.name: clientId
values.description: Client unique identifier, this is not a name of the client; so if you fail you need to try again with client id; can be empty

nodes.notes: This toolHttpRequest node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: DateConverter

parameters.name: DateToMilisecondsConvertorTool
parameters.jsCode:
// Example: convert the incoming query to uppercase and return it
return (new Date(query)).getTime()
parameters.description:
Call this tool to convert dates from format to miliseconds.
Example 2024-12-02T21:04:23.623Z date is converted into time miliseconds.

this ideally is combinated with calculator to calculate duration periods.

nodes.notes: This toolCode node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: ClockifyBlockia

options.systemMessage:
=You are an smart assistant assisting engineers in the time logging management on Clockify for an agency called Blockia Labs using conversational chat system. Be precise and concise, you are engineers best friend copilot.
Your goals is to guide and help engineers in the logging process; by proactively guiding them one step again in the process and giving them options and proactive guidance, not passively waiting only on instructions.

Note that todays day is {{ $now.toUTC() }}

For date duration calculation use the date convertor tool along with the calculator.
Use step by step guidance and reasoning for this process.

For creates, updates and delete operations always double confirm with the user. Especially when deleting, make the confirmation one by one since deleting is critical ops.

Make sure the descriptions are checked, ethic and gramatically correct. 
Make sure there is no overlap in time entries or logging when one user is working on more projects at the same time. 
Return a simple mardown resposne (no bold or ****)

nodes.notes: This agent node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.notes: This slackTrigger node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Execution Data
nodes.notes: This executionData node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.notes: This memoryBufferWindow node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Add reaction
nodes.notes: This slack node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Send reply
nodes.notes: This slack node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Slack Trigger".
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Calculator" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Create New Time Entry" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "GetClientsTool" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get All Time Entries" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Current loggedin user" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "GetProjectsTool" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Update Time Entry" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Delete Time Entry" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "DateConverter" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "ClockifyBlockia" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Execution Data" node (n8n-nodes-base.executionData) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Window Buffer Memory" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Select the Channel or User ID where message notifications should be delivered.
Select the Channel or User ID where message notifications should be delivered.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Your Slack channel will receive customized real-time notifications when the automation executes.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
